channel.title: The Daily — Andrew Park
channel.description: Edition No. 2 — sports, AI, markets, and the 2026 midterms.

item.title: Wings vs. Fire Preview: 3 things to watch as Dallas is on the road again
item.description: Following a 85-70 win fueled by Paige Bueckers on Thursday night, the Dallas Wings (8-4) return to the road to face the Portland Fire (6-8) on Saturday night. Tipoff is at 7:30 p.m. at the Moda Center. While Dallas has won three out of…

item.title: Trade Talk: Helping the Thunder in exchange for a pick
item.description: As things currently stand, the Dallas Mavericks have two picks in the first round of the NBA Draft later this month, namely their own #9 and the Oklahoma City Thunder’s #30. In what is clearly a deep draft, the Mavs would do well to get…

item.title: NBA draft 2026: Six big trade offers, featuring 11 lottery picks
item.description: A historic top-2 swap, a calculated move for OKC and a Giannis blockbuster headline six offers that would rock draft night.

item.title: Kyrie Irving may not be on the trading block despite Mavs’ rebuild
item.description: Earlier this month, we touched on the possibility of the Dallas Mavericks moving veterans Daniel Gafford and Kyrie Irving this offseason as the team looks to rebuild for the future with Cooper Flagg as its centerpiece. In many ways, those…

item.title: Isaiah Evans’ Biggest Fan Is No Surprise
item.description: Isaiah Evans has had a nice few years. First, he was North Carolina’s Gatorade Player of the Year in 2023 and 2024, and then spent two years at Duke. In his first season, the Blue Devils got to the Final Four, and this spring, his…

item.title: YouTube Gold: Duke’s Smartest Point Guard Ever?
item.description: Duke has had some amazing point guards over the years: the elegant Tommy Amaker, the steady and stoic Chris Duhon, the stunning defender Tre Jones, and the cerebral Jon Scheyer among them. Of all of them, our favorite was Bobby Hurley,…

item.title: Cameron Boozer News
item.description: The NBA Draft is less than two weeks away, so it won’t be long until we learn where Cameron Boozer will play ball for the foreseeable future. The smart people now think he may end up being taken by Utah with the second pick, but the smart…

item.title: At NBPA Top 100 Camp, top boys high school recruits embrace a new reality
item.description: In the transfer portal era, college coaches are pitching roles, development and winning rather than immediate star status.

item.title: Braves' Spencer Strider leaves start after significant dip in velocity
item.description: The Braves said that Strider left his start against the Mets with right arm soreness.

item.title: Brighton make £30m offer for Tottenham defender Luka Vuskovic - sources
item.description: Brighton & Hove Albion have made a £30 million ($40.2m) offer for Tottenham Hotspur defender Luka Vuskovic, sources have told ESPN.

item.title: Watch Stanley Cup Final Game 6 on Sunday at 8 p.m. ET on ABC and ESPN App
item.description: Watch Stanley Cup Final Game 6 on Sunday at 8 p.m. ET on ABC and ESPN App

item.title: DeepSeek hiring points to AI infrastructure ambitions beyond rented compute - digitimes
item.description: DeepSeek hiring points to AI infrastructure ambitions beyond rented compute digitimes

item.title: TCS and Anthropic partner to bring Claude to regulated industries
item.description: Anthropic

item.title: We’re launching Claude Corps, a national fellowship program for people early in their careers who are passionate about extending the benefits of AI to communities across America.
item.description: Anthropic

item.title: How Preply combines AI and human tutors to personalize learning
item.description: Preply uses OpenAI to launch AI-generated lesson summaries, providing personalised feedback and language learning exercises.

item.title: Musk’s xAI fired engineer for raising concerns about Grok chatbot, lawsuit claims - The Guardian
item.description: Musk’s xAI fired engineer for raising concerns about Grok chatbot, lawsuit claims The Guardian

item.title: The great AI Irony: China cracks down on Western models while US companies flock to DeepSeek - TechRadar
item.description: The great AI Irony: China cracks down on Western models while US companies flock to DeepSeek TechRadar

item.title: New OpenAI Academy courses for the next era of work
item.description: OpenAI introduces three Academy courses that help people build practical AI skills, create repeatable workflows, and apply agents in everyday work.

item.title: The US government has issued an export control directive to suspend all access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5.
item.description: Anthropic

item.title: ‘This is not a flash in the pan’: Why value stocks are beating growth by such a wide margin
item.description: Value stocks are putting up big gains this year that widely surpass growth equities, with investors appearing optimistic about earnings growth broadening beyond technology.

item.title: Financial Services Roundup: Market Talk
item.description: Find insight on Lennar, SpaceX’s IPO, cryptocurrencies and more in the latest Market Talks covering Financial Services.

item.title: Musk Is the World’s First Trillionaire. Who Was the First Billionaire?
item.description: John D. Rockefeller, the Gilded Age oil baron, became America’s first billionaire in 1916. Like Elon Musk’s ascension, the milestone made headlines.

item.title: Justice Dept. Clears Way for Paramount-Warner Bros. Merger
item.description: The $111 billion deal would unite two major movie studios and put CNN under the same roof as CBS News.

item.title: Stocks Climb on SpaceX’s Debut
item.description: Plus, Elon Musk becomes a trillionaire, and wearables may not be healthy for your portfolio.

item.title: JPMorgan Chase Has a Key Role for SpaceX’s IPO: Party Host
item.description: Branded tomahawk steaks and “The Future is Public” cocktails will be served to guests.
